metadata.title: Event Worker (Outbox → Redis Stream + Read Models)

story.iWant: an Event Worker that drains the transactional outbox, publishes events to Redis Stream, and updates denormalized read models
story.soThat: downstream consumers (SSE gateway, Search Indexer) receive a reliable event stream and users see real-time updates via materialized views with <200ms p95 query latency

subtasks.subtask: Create `OutboxPoller` @Component with @Profile("worker")
Add @Scheduled(fixedDelay = ${synergyflow.outbox.polling.fixed-delay-ms:100})
Query `OutboxRepository.findUnprocessedBatch(limit)` - SELECT * FROM outbox WHERE processed_at IS NULL ORDER BY id LIMIT ?
For each event: call RedisStreamPublisher.publish() + ReadModelUpdater.update() + mark processed
Add Micrometer metrics: outbox_lag_rows (gauge), outbox_processing_time_ms (histogram)
Structured logging: INFO on batch completion with batch_size, processing_time_ms, lag_rows
Create `RedisStreamPublisher` @Component with Redisson RStream client
Publish to stream "domain-events" with maxlen 100000 (24-48h retention)
Serialize OutboxEvent envelope to JSON
Add @Retryable with maxAttempts=3, backoff exponential (1s, 2s, 4s) for transient Redis failures
Add metrics: outbox_events_published_total (counter with event_type tag), outbox_redis_publish_errors_total
Structured logging: WARN on retry attempts, ERROR if all retries exhausted
Create `ReadModelUpdater` @Service that routes events by event_type to handlers
Create `TicketCardHandler`: handle TicketCreated (INSERT ticket_card), TicketAssigned (UPDATE ticket_card SET assignee_name=?, updated_at=?, version=? WHERE ticket_id=? AND version < ?)
Create `IssueCardHandler`: handle IssueCreated (INSERT queue_row + issue_card), IssueStateChanged (UPDATE issue_card SET status=?, updated_at=?, version=? WHERE issue_id=? AND version < ?)
Create JPA entities if not exists: TicketCard, QueueRow, IssueCard (map to ticket_card, queue_row, issue_card tables from V4 migration)
Idempotency: Use version check in WHERE clause to prevent duplicate updates
Add metrics: outbox_read_model_updates_total (counter with event_type, model_name tags), outbox_read_model_update_errors_total
Error handling: Log ERROR if read model update fails, increment error metric, but still mark event as processed (to avoid blocking queue)
Create `GapDetector` @Component with @Profile("worker")
Add @Scheduled(fixedRate = 60000) - run every 1 minute
Query OutboxRepository.findMaxProcessedId() and OutboxRepository.findGaps(maxProcessedId)
SQL: SELECT id FROM outbox WHERE id < ? AND processed_at IS NULL ORDER BY id - detect missing IDs
If gaps found: Log ERROR with gap_start_id, gap_end_id, gap_size; increment outbox_gaps_detected_total metric
Don't block processing - gaps could be in-flight transactions
Create application-worker.yml with spring.profiles.active=worker
Disable web server: spring.autoconfigure.exclude=[WebMvcAutoConfiguration]
Configure polling: synergyflow.outbox.polling.fixed-delay-ms=100, batch-size=100
Configure Redis: spring.redis.stream.domain-events (maxlen, consumer-group, consumer-name)
Configure retry: synergyflow.outbox.retry (max-attempts=3, initial-interval-ms=1000, multiplier=2.0)
Add Redisson dependency to build.gradle.kts: org.redisson:redisson-spring-boot-starter
Create `OutboxPollerIntegrationTest` with Testcontainers (PostgreSQL + Redis)
Test: Insert outbox event → worker polls → verify Redis Stream receives event → verify read model updated → verify processed_at set
Test idempotency: Process same event twice → verify single read model row (version prevents duplicate update)
Test gap detection: Insert events with missing IDs (e.g., 1, 2, 4, 5) → verify gap alert (ID 3 missing)
Test Redis failure retry: Mock Redis failure → verify 3 retry attempts → verify event remains unprocessed
Test read model update failure: Simulate constraint violation → verify event still marked processed, error metric incremented

acceptanceCriteria.ac: Event Worker polls `outbox` table WHERE `processed_at IS NULL` in FIFO order (ORDER BY id) with configurable batch size (default 100 events), using @Scheduled(fixedDelay = 100) for 100ms polling interval
For each unprocessed event, worker publishes event envelope to Redis Stream (topic: `domain-events`) with retry logic (max 3 attempts, exponential backoff 1s/2s/4s) for transient Redis failures

Worker updates corresponding read models based on event type: TicketCreated → INSERT ticket_card, TicketAssigned → UPDATE ticket_card, IssueCreated → INSERT queue_row + issue_card, IssueStateChanged → UPDATE issue_card. Updates are idempotent (version-based: WHERE version < ?)

After successful Redis publish and read model update, worker marks event as processed (UPDATE outbox SET processed_at = now() WHERE id = ?). If Redis publish fails after retries, event remains unprocessed for next poll

Gap detection runs every 60 seconds (@Scheduled(fixedRate = 60000)), detects missing sequence numbers between max processed ID and current max ID, logs ERROR and increments outbox_gaps_detected_total metric if gaps found
Worker is idempotent - replaying an already-processed event (version ≤ current) results in 0 rows affected for UPDATE operations, preventing duplicate side effects
Worker runs as Spring Boot application with @Profile("worker"), no HTTP server enabled (web autoconfiguration excluded), exposes only actuator endpoints for health checks and metrics

Observability in place: outbox_lag_rows (gauge), outbox_processing_time_ms (histogram), outbox_events_published_total (counter), outbox_read_model_updates_total (counter), outbox_read_model_update_errors_total (counter), outbox_gaps_detected_total (counter), outbox_redis_publish_errors_total (counter); structured logging includes batch_size, processing_time_ms, lag_rows

doc.relevance: Defines Event Worker deployment, responsibilities, technology stack, and success criteria

docs.doc: Event Worker is a companion service that polls transactional outbox, publishes to Redis Stream, updates read models. Deployment: 2 replicas, 0.5 CPU/1GB RAM, autoscale on outbox lag. Success criteria: p95 event propagation ≤2s

doc.relevance: Defines read model tables (ticket_card, queue_row, issue_card, sprint_summary) updated by Event Worker

docs.doc: Read models are denormalized views optimized for hot reads (<200ms p95). Event Worker updates materialized views: ticket_card (ITSM queue), queue_row (agent work queue), issue_card (PM board), sprint_summary (velocity tracking)

doc.relevance: Defines OutboxPoller polling logic, RedisStreamPublisher, ReadModelUpdater, GapDetector

docs.doc: OutboxPoller polls every 100ms (fixedDelay), processes batch of 100 events. RedisStreamPublisher uses Redisson RStream. ReadModelUpdater routes events by event_type. GapDetector checks for missing IDs every 60s

doc.relevance: Defines outbox table structure, envelope format, delivery semantics

docs.doc: Outbox schema: aggregate_id, aggregate_type, event_type, version, occurred_at, payload (JSONB), processed_at. Envelope uses lowercase_with_underscores keys. Delivery: at-least-once, consumers must reduce idempotently

doc.relevance: Defines Redis Stream fan-out pattern for SSE and indexers

docs.doc: Event Worker publishes to Redis Stream `sf.events` with message key {aggregateId}:{version}. Consumer groups: app-sse (SSE gateway), read-model (read model updaters), indexer (OpenSearch)

doc.relevance: Defines metrics for outbox publishing (implemented in Story 1.5)

docs.doc: Story 1.5 metrics: outbox_published_total, outbox_publish_errors_total, outbox_publish_latency_ms. Story 1.6 adds: outbox_lag_rows, outbox_processing_time_ms, outbox_events_published_total, outbox_read_model_updates_total

doc.relevance: Defines version-based deduplication strategy for event consumers

docs.doc: Consumers maintain {aggregate_id → version} and drop events with version ≤ current. Read model updaters use WHERE version < ? to ensure idempotent updates
E2E event propagation ≤2s p95 from database commit to client UI update. Reconnection catch-up <1s. Idempotent client reducers drop stale updates by version

doc.relevance: Performance requirement for read model queries

docs.doc: Queue/list operations <200ms p95 (enabled by denormalized read models). Composite side-panel hydration <300ms p95

artifact.relevance: Needs new methods: findUnprocessedBatch(limit), findMaxProcessedId(), findGaps(maxId)

code.artifact: Currently has only basic JpaRepository methods. Story 1.6 needs to add custom query methods for polling unprocessed events and gap detection

artifact.relevance: JPA entity for outbox table, used by OutboxPoller to fetch events

code.artifact: Maps to outbox table (V3 migration). Fields: id (Long), aggregateId (UUID), aggregateType, eventType, eventPayload (JSONB), version, occurredAt, processedAt

artifact.relevance: Event envelope structure for JSON serialization, used by RedisStreamPublisher

code.artifact: Immutable record with stable JSON field names (lowercase_with_underscores). Fields: aggregate_id, aggregate_type, event_type, version, occurred_at, schema_version, payload. CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION = 1

artifact.relevance: Creates outbox table with indexes for polling and gap detection

code.artifact: Outbox table: id BIGSERIAL PK, aggregate_id/aggregate_type/event_type, event_payload JSONB, version, occurred_at, processed_at, retry_count. Indexes: idx_outbox_idempotency (aggregate_id, version UNIQUE), idx_outbox_unprocessed (occurred_at WHERE processed_at IS NULL)

artifact.relevance: Creates read model tables updated by ReadModelUpdater

code.artifact: Creates ticket_card (lines 6-30), queue_row (lines 78-98), issue_card (lines 101-125), sprint_summary (lines 128-152). Each has version column for idempotent updates

architectural.constraint: Use Polling Publisher pattern (100ms fixed delay) instead of CDC. Acceptable for <10K events/hour workload. Can switch to PostgreSQL LISTEN/NOTIFY if lag becomes issue
Read models are denormalized for hot reads (<200ms p95). Event-driven updates maintain eventual consistency. Acceptable lag: <2s p95 from write to read model (NFR9)
Version-based deduplication ensures idempotency. UPDATE statements use WHERE version < ? to prevent replaying old events. At-least-once delivery guarantees from transactional outbox
Event Worker is separate deployment (Spring profile "worker") to isolate physics-heavy event processing from HTTP p95 latency. No HTTP server enabled. Autoscale based on outbox_lag_rows metric

All components in eventing/internal/worker package: OutboxPoller, RedisStreamPublisher, ReadModelUpdater, GapDetector, handlers. Only activated with @Profile("worker"). No dependencies on itsm/pm domain logic - worker uses event payload only (decoupled)

Each event processed in its own transaction (Redis publish + read model update + mark processed). If Redis publish succeeds but read model update fails → event still marked processed (acceptable - read models are eventually consistent)

errorHandling.constraint: Redis connection failure: Use @Retryable with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, max 3 attempts). If all retries fail: Log ERROR, increment outbox_redis_publish_errors_total, leave event unprocessed (will retry on next poll). Don't mark processed_at if publish fails
Read model update failure (constraint violation, FK error): Log ERROR with event details, increment outbox_read_model_update_errors_total. Mark event as processed anyway (to avoid blocking queue)

Gap detected in sequence: Log ERROR with gap range (gap_start_id, gap_end_id, gap_size). Alert via metrics spike (outbox_gaps_detected_total counter). Don't block processing - gaps could be in-flight transaction commits
Database connection failure during polling: Spring @Scheduled will retry on next interval (fixedDelay = 100ms). Log WARN

method.description: Query unprocessed events: SELECT * FROM outbox WHERE processed_at IS NULL ORDER BY id LIMIT ? - AC1
Query max processed ID: SELECT MAX(id) FROM outbox WHERE processed_at IS NOT NULL - AC5
Detect gaps: SELECT id FROM outbox WHERE id < ? AND processed_at IS NULL ORDER BY id - AC5
Publish event envelope to Redis Stream 'domain-events' with retry logic - AC2
Route event to appropriate handler (TicketCardHandler, IssueCardHandler) based on event_type - AC3

tests.standards: Integration tests using Spring Boot Test + Testcontainers (PostgreSQL 16 + Redis 7). Test transactional semantics with real databases. All worker components tested with @Profile("worker") active. Micrometer metrics verified via MeterRegistry. Structured logging verified via Logback test appender

ideas.idea: Test OutboxPoller: Insert outbox event → worker polls → verify event fetched in FIFO order → verify batch size limit respected
Test RedisStreamPublisher: Publish event → verify Redis Stream receives event with correct envelope structure → test retry on Redis connection failure (3 attempts)
Test ReadModelUpdater: TicketCreated event → verify INSERT into ticket_card → TicketAssigned event → verify UPDATE ticket_card (idempotent WHERE version < ?)
Test ReadModelUpdater: IssueCreated event → verify INSERT into queue_row AND issue_card → IssueStateChanged event → verify UPDATE issue_card
Test marking processed: After successful publish + update → verify processed_at timestamp set → verify event not fetched on next poll
Test Redis failure: Mock Redis failure → verify event remains unprocessed (processed_at IS NULL) → verify next poll retries same event
Test GapDetector: Insert events with IDs 1, 2, 4, 5 (missing 3) → verify gap detection logs ERROR → verify outbox_gaps_detected_total metric incremented
Test idempotency: Process same event twice → verify single row in read model → verify second UPDATE affects 0 rows (WHERE version < ? prevents duplicate)

Test observability: Verify all metrics present (outbox_lag_rows, outbox_processing_time_ms, outbox_events_published_total, outbox_read_model_updates_total, outbox_read_model_update_errors_total, outbox_gaps_detected_total, outbox_redis_publish_errors_total) → verify structured logging includes batch_size, processing_time_ms, lag_rows
